sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after RIGHT. Let me compare the before and after frames to see what moved. I need to check if the black avatar object moved, or if something else changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after RIGHT action
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

# Find the black avatar (node with white children) before and after
def find_avatar(nodes):
    for n in nodes:
        if n['children'] and all(c['color'] == 'W' for c in [nodes[x] for x in n['children']]):
            b = n['boundary']
            return (n['id'], n['color'], n['hash'], min([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b]))
    return None

def find_by_color_hash(nodes, color, hash_val):
    for n in nodes:
        if n['color'] == color and n['hash'] == hash_val:
            b = n['boundary']
            return (n['id'], min([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b]), n['pixels'])
    return None

before_avatar = find_avatar(before)
after_avatar = find_avatar(after)
print(f"Before avatar: {before_avatar}")
print(f"After avatar: {after_avatar}")

# Check all objects for position changes
print("\nAll objects position comparison:")
for n in after:
    b = n['boundary']
    r, c = min([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b])
    # Find matching hash in before
    for bn in before:
        if bn['hash'] == n['hash'] and bn['id'] != n['id']:
            bb = bn['boundary']
            br, bc = min([p[0] for p in bb]), min([p[1] for p in bb])
            if r != br or c != bc:
                print(f"  Node {n['id']} (hash={n['hash'][:8]}): moved from ({br},{bc}) to ({r},{c})")
            break
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
